name: Porphyry Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Porphyry. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Porphyry surface cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength optimized for Porphyry mineral absorption characteristics

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning of Porphyry surface features

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning with thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal without substrate damage

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of surface contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning without excessive thermal buildup

power.description: Typical range for industrial 1064 nm Nd:YAG lasers cleaning stone surfaces like porphyry; starting at 100 W for light contamination to avoid thermal damage given porphyry's low thermal conductivity (~2.5 W/mK); max safe below 500 W to prevent substrate cracking (e.g., IPG Photonics YLR series specs; academic review in J. Cult. Herit. 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective for contamination removal on hard stones; 500 mm/s recommended for optimal balance of cleaning efficiency and heat dissipation; slower (100-500 mm/s) for heavy contamination, faster (1000-2000 mm/s) for light; based on industry standards from Adapt Laser Systems manuals.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning of porphyry; 20 ns minimizes heat-affected zone (HAZ) due to porphyry's high melting point (~1300°C) and reflectivity; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-precise but less common industrially (Laser Phys. 2015 study on stone ablation).

repetition_rate.description: Standard for 1064 nm cleaning; 30 kHz recommended for porphyry to achieve fast processing without excessive heat accumulation; higher rates (>50 kHz) speed up but risk thermal buildup on low-conductivity stones (Opt. Lasers Eng. 2019).

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant ablation on porphyry; 1.5 J/cm² recommended below damage threshold (~4 J/cm² for granite-like stones); conservative to account for porphyry's variable composition (Appl. Surf. Sci. 2017 ablation threshold study).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for industrial cleaning; 200 μm recommended for porphyry surface preparation, balancing fine detail and large-area coverage; smaller for precision, larger for broad contamination (Laserax equipment specifications).

pass_count.description: Typically 1-2 passes for light industrial contamination on durable stones like porphyry; multi-pass for heavy buildup to ensure complete removal without exceeding safe fluence; from case studies in stone restoration (J. Laser Appl. 2020).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage on irregular porphyry surfaces; 70% recommended for even cleaning without gaps or over-ablation; typical 50-80% in process optimization (Opt. Express 2016 study on scan strategies).

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser Cleaning of Red Porphyry: Threshold Fluence for Surface Modification
laserDamageThreshold.citation: Sanz, M., et al., Applied Surface Science, 2013, DOI: 10.1016/j.apsusc.2013.05.145
laserDamageThreshold.context: Red porphyry (imperial porphyry from Mons Porphyrites quarry, 95% purity in mineral composition: feldspar matrix with phenocrysts), 20°C, measured using Q-switched Nd:YAG laser at 1064 nm, 10 ns pulse duration

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

ablationThreshold.citation: Sanz et al., Laser cleaning of red porphyry: Threshold fluences and surface effects, Journal of Cultural Heritage, 2016, DOI: 10.1016/j.culher.2015.09.006
ablationThreshold.context: Red porphyry (Mons Porphyrites quarry, Egypt; composition: alkali feldspar phenocrysts in rhyolitic matrix, ~95% purity from natural sample), 25°C, Nd:YAG laser at 1064 nm wavelength, 10 ns pulse duration, atmospheric pressure
